name: Hadramawt
description: Shibam's 16th-century mudbrick high-rises reach 11 storeys — the world's oldest skyscrapers. The valley also contains Tarim's palatial mosques and Seiyun's whitewashed towers. UNESCO World Heritage 1982.
traditional: Sun-dried mudbrick tower, battered (tapered) walls, flat roof with parapet, raised flood platform.
transitional: Mudbrick form at 4–6 storeys with modern reinforcement and drainage.
contemporary: Contemporary Hadramawt: mudbrick cladding system, vertical proportions, earth-tone palette.
